title: Ipomoea verrucipes

This family includes over 1,800 species of flowering plants, commonly known as morning glories. The plant is native to North America and is often found in moist, woodland environments. It is a perennial herb with heart-shaped leaves and white, funnel-shaped flowers.

side_effects: Ipomoea verrucipes is a plant native to the southern United States, and its seeds contain a toxic compound called coniine. This compound is known to be toxic to humans and animals if ingested in large amounts. Symptoms of coniine poisoning can include nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as more severe effects such as muscle weakness and respiratory failure. intro: Ipomoea verrucipes, also known as wild sweet potato vine, is a perennial climbing plant native to the southeastern United States.
